design_philosophy.core_loop: Place falling Tetris pieces to clear lines; hybrid mechanic blending Tetris block-placement with Dr. Mario viral clearing concept
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. spatial decision-making; managing falling pieces while planning board state
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master classic puzzle mechanics through execution and planning; experience a familiar game with a new twist
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentional fusion of two Nintendo puzzle IP (Tetris + Dr. Mario logic); positioned as spiritual successor to NES Tetris rather than direct mechanical sequel; regional presentation differences suggest experimentation with audience expectations

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: CPU difficulty levels present (referenced via stone tablet graphics), suggesting selectable difficulty modes for player agency

progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty selection via CPU opponent levels
replayability_hooks: Regional title screen variants suggest different game modes between JP/US/EU versions

world_building_techniques: Animated title screen in Japanese version creates distinct presentation identity
Static title screen in international versions emphasizes mechanical focus over narrative
Unused cutscene theme suggests aborted narrative framing (present in ROM but unused)

environmental_storytelling: Regional presentation differences (animated JP vs. static international) signal cultural/market differentiation
Hybrid Tetris/Dr. Mario concept positions game as Nintendo's proprietary puzzle franchise evolution

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Tetris 2 (NES) article
data_tier_notes: T3 - Empirical/qualitative data extracted. Sources focus on development metadata, regional differences, and unused assets rather than mechanical documentation. Core gameplay loop inferred from 'mish-mash of Tetris and Dr. Mario' characterization.
limitations: Limited mechanical detail in sources; boss design section empty (puzzle game paradigm does not employ traditional boss encounters); difficulty philosophy inferred from presence of CPU difficulty levels rather than explicitly documented
